model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: UConn is the vastly superior team as a 2-seed with a 27-4 regular season record and Big East championship, while Furman is a 15-seed from the Southern Conference with a 22-12 record and significantly weaker analytical profile (KenPom rank 191 vs. UConn's elite status). The Huskies' elite defensive efficiency and tournament experience under Dan Hurley should overwhelm the Paladins, who have not faced a team of UConn's caliber all season. The 20.5-point betting line and -5000 moneyline reflect the massive talent disparity, making a 15-over-2 upset extremely unlikely.

reasoningStep.title: UConn vs Furman Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: UConn is a dominant 2-seed facing a significant underdog in 15-seed Furman, with overwhelming statistical and talent advantages.
reasoningStep.evidence: UConn went 27-4 in the regular season, won the Big East, and is a 20.5-point favorite with -5000 moneyline odds. Furman has a KenPom rank of 191 with a 22-12 record from the Southern Conference, while UConn's elite defensive efficiency and tournament experience under Dan Hurley provide a decisive edge. Historical 2 vs. 15 matchups heavily favor the higher seed, and Furman lacks the offensive firepower (rank 200 in adjusted offense) to challenge UConn's defense.

Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI UConn vs. Furman: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for UConn vs. Furman. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: UConn vs. Furman Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. UConn 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Dan Hurley’s team regrouped well after its second-round exit last March. The Huskies went 27-4 in the regular season and reclaimed the Big East summit with a 17-3 conference record. Their 72-40 dismantling of St. John’s was the first time UConn held a Big East opponent to 20% shooting or worse in more than 30 seasons, and it said everything about UConn's ceiling. Alex Karaban is the senior heartbeat of this team. He moved into the top 10 in program history in career scoring during the regular-season finale and scored 23 points on senior day to will his team to a comeback win over Seton Hall. Tarris Reed Jr. is as dominant a rebounder as there is in college basketball, anchoring an interior defense that ranks top 10 nationally in opponent two-point shooting and block percentage. Solo Ball has done well in his second full season as a starter, averaging 13.5 points per game, and freshman Braylon Mullins has given UConn a perimeter weapon to go with Silas Demary Jr., who owns a top-15 assist rate nationally. The Huskies rank top 10 in assists per game and assist-to-field goal percentage. Nothing goes to waste. There are holes to poke, as losses to Creighton and Marquette were confusing red flags for the tournament. The Huskies do a lot of things really well, but it's hard to say they do anything the best. Basket Under Review released a “Kemba Factor” ranking for potential takeover players in March, and UConn’s highest-rated player was Demary at No. 43. When the going gets tough, it's hard to see anyone as a go-to bucket-getter. Karaban can knock down a shot anywhere off a screen bu

Overall Team W   L   PCT W   L   PCT No. 10 St. John's † 18 – 2   .900 28 – 6   .824 No. 7 UConn 17 – 3   .850 29 – 5   .853 Villanova 15 – 5   .750 24 – 8   .750 Seton Hall 10 – 10   .500 21 – 12   .636 Creighton 9 – 11   .450 15 – 17   .469 DePaul 8 – 12   .400 16 – 16   .500 Marquette 7 – 13   .350 12 – 20   .375 Butler 7 – 13   .350 16 – 16   .500 Providence 7 – 13   .350 15 – 18   .455 Xavier 6 – 14   .300 15 – 18   .455 Georgetown 6 – 14   .300 16 – 18   .471 † 2026 Big East tournament winner As of March 14, 2026 Rankings from AP poll The 2025–26 UConn Huskies men's basketball team represents the University of Connecticut in the 2025–26 NCAA Division I men's basketball season . The Huskies are led by eighth-year head coach Dan Hurley in the team's sixth season since their return to the Big East Conference . The Huskies play their home games at the Harry A. Gampel Pavilion on-campus in Storrs, Connecticut and the PeoplesBank Arena in Hartford, Connecticut . This marked the first season that Tom Moore was not on the coaching staff since his second stint started during the 2018–19 season , moving to a general manager role. Previous season [ edit

The Huskies rank top 10 in assists per game and assist-to-field goal percentage. Nothing goes to waste. There are holes to poke, as losses to Creighton and Marquette were confusing red flags for the tournament. The Huskies do a lot of things really well, but it's hard to say they do anything the best. Basket Under Review released a “Kemba Factor” ranking for potential takeover players in March, and UConn’s highest-rated player was Demary at No. 43. When the going gets tough, it's hard to see anyone as a go-to bucket-getter. Karaban can knock down a shot anywhere off a screen bu

The Huskies are led by eighth-year head coach Dan Hurley in the team's sixth season since their return to the Big East Conference . The Huskies play their home games at the Harry A. Gampel Pavilion on-campus in Storrs, Connecticut and the PeoplesBank Arena in Hartford, Connecticut . This marked the first season that Tom Moore was not on the coaching staff since his second stint started during the 2018–19 season , moving to a general manager role. Previous season [ edit
